contract.purpose: AI-facing authoring contract for JSON-driven SwiftUI screen generation in the UIBuilder runtime.
contract.audience: AI language models generating screen JSON for the UIBuilder runtime.
contract.principle: Semantic nodes are the primary authoring tools. Layout primitives (vstack, hstack, zstack) are fallbacks for custom composition, not the default worldview. Always prefer the highest-level semantic node that correctly expresses the UI intent.

documentStructure.description: Every screen is a ScreenDocument JSON object.

fieldNotes.schema: Must be exactly 'com.ilya.ui/1.0'. Validation fails on any other value.
fieldNotes.screenId: Unique string identifier for the screen.
fieldNotes.root: The root UINode of the screen content tree.
fieldNotes.events: Named event handler map. Must be present even when empty: {}. Key is handler name, value is array of actions.
fieldNotes.title: Display title for the screen (optional, informational).
fieldNotes.theme: Design tokens: spacing, cornerRadius, colors map (name → hex), fonts map. Optional.
fieldNotes.state: Initial state variables. state.vars is a map of key → initial value (string, number, bool). Declare every key used in bind props here.

uiNode.description: A single UI node in the content tree.

fieldNotes.type: Node type name (see nodeLibrary). Unknown types render a warning placeholder.
fieldNotes.id: Unique node identifier within the document. Required for nodes referenced by toolbaritem lifting or state keys.
fieldNotes.props: Key-value map of node-specific and style properties.
fieldNotes.children: Array of child UINode objects. Only allowed when the node permits children.
fieldNotes.visibleWhen: Expression evaluated at render time. Node is hidden (not removed) when false.
fieldNotes.enabledWhen: Expression evaluated at render time. Node interactions are disabled when false.
fieldNotes.event: Maps event trigger names (e.g. 'tap', 'change') to handler names defined in top-level events.
fieldNotes.items: Optional list items array for list-based nodes. Less common than children.

navigationstack.purpose: Establishes a navigation context. Provides a navigation bar, a screen title, and a toolbar slot. Wraps all non-toolbar children as vertical content.
rendererOwns: Separating toolbaritem children from content children automatically.
Wrapping content children in a VStack with spacing=0.
Applying .navigationTitle() from the title prop.
Mounting toolbaritem children into the SwiftUI toolbar via .toolbar().
authorMustProvide: The title prop when a navigation bar title is desired.
toolbaritem children for each toolbar button needed.
Content children as direct children (not wrapped in an extra VStack).
useWhen: The screen has a navigation bar with a visible title.
The screen needs toolbar buttons (add, edit, done, share, etc.).
The screen is a top-level destination that requires platform navigation chrome.
A tab page needs its own navigation bar and toolbar.
avoidWhen: The screen is a modal or sheet without a navigation bar.
The screen is already wrapped in a parent navigationstack (do not nest).
The screen only needs a title as a text node — use a text node instead.

childConstraints.toolbaritem: Direct children of type toolbaritem are lifted into the toolbar. All other children become the navigation content.
childConstraints.note: Do not manually wrap content children in a vstack — the renderer adds it.

toolbaritem.purpose: Defines a button or control in the navigation bar or bottom bar. Must be a direct child of navigationstack to have effect. Children become the toolbar content.
rendererOwns: Lifting this node into the SwiftUI toolbar when used inside navigationstack.
Grouping children using ToolbarItemGroup with the specified placement.
authorMustProvide: placement prop to specify where in the toolbar the item appears.
At least one button child as the toolbar item content.
useWhen: Adding an action button to the navigation bar (add, edit, done, filter, etc.).
Placing multiple related buttons in the trailing or leading bar position.
avoidWhen: Outside of navigationstack — children will render inline without toolbar behavior.
As a child of a vstack or hstack — it has no toolbar semantics there.

list.purpose: Platform-styled vertical scrollable list of content rows. Renders with native iOS list appearance: separators, inset groups, swipe actions. section children group rows under headers.
rendererOwns: List styling including row separators and inset grouping.
Vertical scroll behavior (list is already scrollable — do not wrap in scroll).
Platform-appropriate row spacing and dividers.
authorMustProvide: Content children: label, toggle, textfield, button, or section nodes.
section children to group rows under a named header when grouping is needed.
useWhen: Displaying a settings-style screen with labeled rows.
Showing a vertical list of items (contacts, tasks, results, options).
The content is a vertically scrollable collection of similar rows.
Section grouping is needed (Account, Support, Preferences sections, etc.).
Platform-standard list chrome is desired.
avoidWhen: The layout is custom and non-row-based — use vstack instead.
The content is horizontal — use hstack or scroll with axis=horizontal.
The screen has very few items with custom spacing — vstack may be cleaner.

childConstraints.note: section children create grouped sections. Any UINode is technically valid as a child.

section.purpose: Semantic grouping of rows under an optional header title. Fits naturally inside list. Renders as a SwiftUI Section with a visible header when title is provided.
rendererOwns: Rendering the section header from the title prop.
Platform-appropriate section grouping visual treatment.
authorMustProvide: title prop when a header label is desired.
Content children as the section rows.
useWhen: Grouping related rows inside a list under a named heading.
Creating collapsible or named groups within a settings or content list.
Any time rows in a list belong to a named category.
avoidWhen: Outside of a list context — section has no special visual behavior there.
When no grouping is needed — just use flat list children.
As a substitute for group (which is for standalone grouped content).

title.description: Section header label. Omit for an untitled section.

tabview.purpose: Multi-tab navigation container. Renders a SwiftUI TabView with a tab bar. Direct children must be tab nodes. Optionally binds the selected tab to a state key.
rendererOwns: Tab bar rendering.
Tab selection state when a selection binding is provided.
Silently ignoring non-tab children.
authorMustProvide: tab nodes as the only direct children.
id on each tab node when using selection binding (tab.id is the selection value).
selection prop bound to a state key when programmatic tab selection is needed.
useWhen: The screen is a top-level multi-destination view (Home, Search, Profile, Settings).
There are 2–5 primary navigation destinations with tab bar icons.
Tab-based navigation is the primary navigation model for the screen.
avoidWhen: Switching between views that are not primary destinations (use group with collapsible or conditional visibility instead).
Only one 'tab' exists — use the content directly.
Tab bar should be hidden — there is no hide-tab-bar mechanism in the current runtime.

selection.description: State key bound to the selected tab's id.

childConstraints.required: All children must be of type tab.

tab.purpose: A single tab page inside tabview. Carries the tab bar label (title + icon). Children are the tab page content.
rendererOwns: Applying .tabItem modifier with Label(title, icon) or Text(title) to the page content.
Rendering children in a VStack.
authorMustProvide: title prop for the tab bar label.
icon prop (SF Symbol name) for the tab bar icon.
id that matches the selection binding value used in the parent tabview.
useWhen: As a direct child of tabview to represent a named tab destination.
avoidWhen: Outside of tabview — children will render as a plain VStack without tab semantics.

icon.description: SF Symbol name for the tab bar icon.

group.purpose: Standalone semantic content grouping with optional title, footer, and collapsible behavior. Not a list row group — use section inside list for that. Supports visual styles: plain, card, inset.
rendererOwns: Rendering the title headline above children.
Rendering the footer caption below children.
Wrapping children in DisclosureGroup when collapsible=true.
Applying card or inset visual treatment based on the style prop.
Managing expanded/collapsed state using node id as the state key suffix.
authorMustProvide: title for labeled groups.
style prop when card or inset visual treatment is desired.
collapsible=true and a node id when the group should expand/collapse.
Content children.
useWhen: Grouping related form fields or UI elements with a visible title.
Presenting a card-style contained region (style='card').
Creating an expand/collapse section outside of a list (collapsible=true).
Presenting an inset-padded group (style='inset').
avoidWhen: Inside a list — use section instead.
When no grouping behavior or title is needed — use vstack.
As a substitute for list rows.

footer.description: Caption text below the group content.

collapsible.description: When true, renders a DisclosureGroup that the user can expand/collapse. Requires a node id.

style.description: Visual style. card adds elevated background and rounded corners. inset adds horizontal padding.

label.purpose: Semantic icon + text unit. Renders as a SwiftUI Label. Author must NOT manually compose hstack + icon + text when a label is needed.
rendererOwns: Correct icon-text layout and spacing per platform conventions.
authorMustProvide: title prop (required): the text content.
icon prop (optional): SF Symbol name for the icon.
useWhen: Displaying an item with an icon and a text label (settings rows, menu items, list rows).
Any time icon + text is a single semantic unit.
Inside list/section for labeled rows.
As children of menu for menu items with icons.
avoidWhen: The item is icon-only — use icon instead.
The item is text-only — use text instead.
The icon and text need independent styling or spacing — use hstack with icon and text children.

title.description: Label text. Accepts a string or a bind expression.

icon.description: SF Symbol name for the label icon.

button.purpose: Interactive action trigger. Renders a SwiftUI Button with optional icon. Fires the 'tap' event when pressed.
rendererOwns: Rendering Label(title, icon) when icon is provided, Text(title) otherwise.
SwiftUI default button styling.
authorMustProvide: label prop: the button title text.
event.tap: the handler name to fire when tapped.
useWhen: User needs to trigger an action (save, delete, submit, navigate, etc.).
A toolbar action button is needed (provide as child of toolbaritem).
A menu item action is needed (provide as child of menu).
avoidWhen: Navigation row behavior is needed — use label inside list/section with a tap event instead.

icon.description: SF Symbol name shown alongside the label.

events.tap: Fires when the button is pressed.

toggle.purpose: Binary on/off switch bound to a boolean state key. Renders as a SwiftUI Toggle.
rendererOwns: Binding the toggle to the state value.
Triggering the 'change' event after state update.
authorMustProvide: title prop: visible label text.
bind prop: the state key holding the boolean value.
useWhen: Enabling or disabling a setting (notifications, dark mode, Wi-Fi, etc.).
Any binary on/off control.
avoidWhen: Multi-state selection — use menu or tabview instead.

bind.description: State key for the boolean value.

events.change: Fires after the toggle state changes.

textfield.purpose: Single-line text input field bound to a string state key. Supports password mode, keyboard type, prefix/suffix icons, and length capping.
rendererOwns: Rendering SecureField when secure=true.
Capping input length at maxLength characters.
Wrapping prefix/suffix icons in an HStack with the field.
Triggering the 'change' event after each keystroke.
authorMustProvide: bind prop: the state key for the string value.
placeholder when user hint text is needed.
The corresponding state key initialized in state.vars.
useWhen: Free-text user input (name, email, search, message).
Password entry (secure=true).
Numeric input (keyboardType='numberPad' or 'decimalPad').
avoidWhen: Multi-line text input — not supported; consider multiple textfield nodes or use text for display.

bind.description: State key for the text value.

secure.description: Renders as SecureField (password input) when true.

keyboardType.note: Use 'email' (not 'emailAddress'), 'phone' (not 'phonePad'). Unknown values fall back to 'default'.

maxLength.description: Maximum character count. Input is capped at this length.

prefixIcon.description: SF Symbol shown before the field.

suffixIcon.description: SF Symbol shown after the field.

events.change: Fires after the text value changes.

slider.purpose: Continuous or stepped value slider bound to a numeric state key. Renders as a SwiftUI Slider.
rendererOwns: Binding the slider value to state.
Triggering the 'change' event after value changes.
Rendering the step-based slider when step is provided.
authorMustProvide: bind prop: the state key for the numeric value.
min and max props defining the value range.
The corresponding state key initialized in state.vars.
useWhen: Selecting a numeric value within a range (volume, brightness, price range, etc.).
Stepped selection (star ratings, quantity selectors with step=1).
avoidWhen: Discrete option selection — use menu or toggle instead.

bind.description: State key for the numeric value.

step.description: Step increment. Omit for continuous slider.

accentColor.description: Hex color for the slider tint.

events.change: Fires after the slider value changes.

menu.purpose: Contextual dropdown menu with a trigger button and action items. The trigger label is defined via props (title + icon) or a special label child. All other children are menu items.
rendererOwns: Identifying the label child (by id='label' or type='label') as the menu trigger.
Rendering remaining children as menu items in the dropdown.
Constructing the trigger as Label(title, icon) when props are provided.
authorMustProvide: title and/or icon props for the trigger button (or a label child with id='label').
button children for each menu item.
useWhen: A button reveals a list of contextual actions (Edit, Delete, Share, etc.).
Overflow actions that do not fit in the main UI.
Secondary actions behind an ellipsis or similar disclosure control.
avoidWhen: Primary single actions — use button instead.
Navigation between screens — use tabview or navigationstack.

icon.description: SF Symbol for the trigger button.

childConstraints.labelChild: A child with id='label' or type='label' is used as the trigger label view instead of title/icon props.
childConstraints.itemChildren: All other children (typically button nodes) are the menu items.

image.purpose: Standalone visual image. Renders a resizable SwiftUI Image from an asset catalog name or an SF Symbol. Always resizable — use width/height style props to constrain size.
rendererOwns: Making the image resizable.
Applying contentMode (fit or fill) via aspectRatio modifier.
authorMustProvide: Either systemName (SF Symbol name) or name (asset catalog name) — mutually exclusive.
width and/or height style props when a specific size is required.
useWhen: Displaying a full-size image or large icon from assets.
Displaying a large SF Symbol as a visual element (not a decorative glyph).
avoidWhen: Small inline SF Symbol glyphs inside text or labels — use icon instead.

systemName.description: SF Symbol name. Mutually exclusive with name.

name.description: Asset catalog image name. Mutually exclusive with systemName.

icon.purpose: Small decorative SF Symbol glyph rendered at a configurable point size. Use for inline icons, not for standalone visual content.
rendererOwns: Rendering Image(systemName:) with the specified font size.
authorMustProvide: name prop: the SF Symbol name.
useWhen: Decorative or informational inline SF Symbol glyph.
Icon in a custom hstack layout where label is not appropriate.
avoidWhen: Icon + text together — use label instead.
Large standalone visual image — use image instead.

text.purpose: Static or dynamic text display. The value prop accepts a plain string or a bind expression. Supports state interpolation: 'Hello {name}' binds the 'name' state key inline.
rendererOwns: Resolving bind expressions and interpolation in the value prop.
Applying font size and weight from props.
authorMustProvide: value prop: the text string or bind expression.
useWhen: Any non-interactive text display.
Displaying a dynamic state-bound value.
avoidWhen: Icon + text combination — use label.

value.description: Text content. Accepts: (1) a plain string, (2) a { 'bind': 'key' } expression to display a state value, (3) a { 'format': 'Hello {name}!', 'binds': ['name'] } object for multi-key interpolation, or (4) a plain string with {key} placeholders when binds are listed.

badge.purpose: Status label displayed in a capsule background. Commonly used for tags, statuses, and count indicators.
rendererOwns: Rendering Text in a blue-tinted Capsule background with caption font.
authorMustProvide: text prop or value prop: the badge text.
useWhen: Tag-style status indicators.
Count or category labels.
Short status strings needing visual emphasis.

shape.purpose: Geometric shape for decorative or structural purposes. Supports fill, stroke, and dashed/dotted stroke styles. Children are rendered on top of the shape.
rendererOwns: Drawing the shape with the specified fill and/or stroke.
Rendering children in a ZStack on top of the shape.
authorMustProvide: type prop: the shape variant.
At least one of fill or strokeColor to make the shape visible.
useWhen: Decorative geometric elements.
Circular avatars or badges (type='circle' with fill).
Dividers, highlight regions, or background shapes.

childConstraints.note: Children are rendered on top of the shape in a ZStack.

table.purpose: Tabular data display with column headers, alternating row backgrounds, and optional selection. Children are rows; section children group rows.
rendererOwns: Rendering column header row from the columns prop.
Applying alternating row background when alternatingRows=true.
Applying row height from rowHeight prop.
authorMustProvide: columns prop: comma-separated column header names.
Row children (typically hstack with one text/badge child per column).
useWhen: Displaying structured data in named columns (leaderboards, data grids, reports).
Data that is inherently tabular with fixed column structure.
avoidWhen: Simple lists of items — use list + section instead.
Data with variable structure — use list rows with hstack instead.

columns.description: Comma-separated column header names, e.g. 'Name, Score, Level'.

scroll.purpose: Scrollable container for content that may overflow the viewport. The renderer automatically wraps children in a VStack — do NOT add a vstack wrapper inside scroll.
rendererOwns: Wrapping children in a VStack (vertical axis) or HStack (horizontal axis) internally.
Applying the padding prop to the internal stack.
authorMustProvide: Content children directly (no extra vstack wrapper).
axis='horizontal' prop for horizontal scrolling.
useWhen: Custom-layout content that may overflow the viewport vertically.
Horizontal scrolling content (axis='horizontal').
A form or long page built from custom vstack/hstack composition.
avoidWhen: Wrapping a list node — list is already scrollable.
Wrapping a tabview — tab content should scroll internally if needed.
Short content that does not need to scroll.

spacing.description: Spacing applied to the internal VStack.

padding.description: Padding applied to the internal VStack.

childConstraints.note: Do NOT add a vstack as the only child of scroll. The renderer inserts a VStack internally.

vstack.purpose: Vertical layout stack. A low-level composition primitive. Use when no semantic container (list, group, section, navigationstack) fits the intent.
rendererOwns: Vertical stacking of children.
Applying alignment and spacing from props.
authorMustProvide: Content children.
useWhen: Custom vertical layout that is not row-based list content.
Mixed content that does not match any semantic container.
Hero sections, banners, or composed form areas.
avoidWhen: The content is a list of rows — use list + section instead.
The screen needs a navigation bar — use navigationstack as root.
Inside scroll as the only child — scroll already provides a VStack.
Inside a list to simulate section groups — use section instead.

hstack.purpose: Horizontal layout stack. A low-level composition primitive for custom horizontal arrangements.
rendererOwns: Horizontal stacking of children.
Applying spacing from props.
authorMustProvide: Content children.
useWhen: Side-by-side layout of different element types.
Table row cells (one per column).
Custom horizontal compositions not covered by label or button.
avoidWhen: Icon + text combination — use label instead.

zstack.purpose: Depth-based overlay stack. Renders children on top of each other. Use for overlaying elements, background/foreground compositions, and badge-over-icon patterns.

frame.purpose: Applies explicit frame constraints and alignment to its children. Rendered as a ZStack with the specified alignment. Use for precise sizing and alignment, not for content grouping.
rendererOwns: Wrapping children in a ZStack with the specified alignment.
Applying frame constraints from style props.
useWhen: Precise width/height constraints on a subtree.
Overriding the default alignment of a child element.
Fixed-size containers.
avoidWhen: Content grouping with a title — use group instead.
Row grouping in a list — use section instead.

spacer.purpose: Flexible whitespace that fills available space. Use inside hstack or vstack to push elements apart.

styleProps.description: Style properties available on all nodes via the props map. Applied by NodeStyle.apply().

maxWidth.description: Maximum width. Use the literal string 'infinity' (not a number) for .infinity — this is the only string value accepted for this prop. Use a number for any other constraint.

foregroundColor.description: Hex color for text and icons, e.g. '#1C1C1E'.

shadowRadius.description: Shadow blur radius. Shadow is only applied when this is set.

notes: Side-specific padding (paddingTop etc.) takes priority over uniform padding.
backgroundColor is applied before clipShape/cornerRadius so the background clips correctly.
Shadow is only rendered when shadowRadius is present.
borderColor renders as a RoundedRectangle overlay — uses cornerRadius if set.

expressions.description: Expression objects used in visibleWhen, enabledWhen, and conditional action fields.

bind.description: References the current value of a state variable.

expressions.literals: Plain JSON values (strings, numbers, booleans) are valid expressions.
usageNotes: visibleWhen: node is hidden (EmptyView) when the expression evaluates to false.
enabledWhen: node interactions are disabled when the expression evaluates to false.
Text simple bind: use { 'bind': 'key' } as the entire value prop to display a single state value.

Text format interpolation: use { 'format': 'Hello {name}, you have {count} messages', 'binds': ['name', 'count'] } to embed multiple state values in a string. The {key} placeholders in format are replaced with the corresponding state values.
There is no escape sequence for literal braces in format strings. If a literal '{' or '}' is needed, use a plain string value without the format object.

actions.description: Action objects in event handler arrays. Each action has a 'do' field specifying the operation.

state.set.description: Sets a state variable to the given value. The runtime stores values as DynamicValue (string, number, bool). Type coercion is permissive — setting a string to a previously-numeric key is allowed but may cause bind inconsistencies. Prefer matching the initial type declared in state.vars.

state.inc.description: Increments a numeric state variable. Optional max cap.

state.dec.description: Decrements a numeric state variable. Optional min floor.

ai.request.description: Sends a prompt to the AI and optionally stores the response in a state key. The prompt string is passed to the AI runtime as-is. Author is responsible for keeping prompts appropriate and bounded in scope.

actions.conditionalExecution: Each action may have a 'when' field with an expression. The action is skipped when the expression evaluates to false.

decisionRules.question: What should be the root container of the screen?

rules.if: Screen needs a navigation bar, navigation title, or toolbar buttons
rules.then: Use navigationstack as root. All screen content goes inside it as children.

rules.if: Screen has multiple primary tab destinations
rules.then: Use tabview as root. Each destination is a tab child.

rules.if: Screen is a form or long content page without navigation chrome
rules.then: Use scroll as root so content is scrollable. Do NOT add a vstack inside scroll.

rules.if: Screen is a settings or options list
rules.then: Use list as root (or inside navigationstack). Add section children for grouping.

rules.if: Screen is a short custom-layout page
rules.then: Use vstack as root.

decisionRules.question: Should I use list or vstack for vertical content?

rules.if: Content is a collection of similar rows (settings, options, items, contacts, tasks)
rules.then: Use list. Add section children for grouping.

rules.if: Content is custom-layout with varied elements and no row structure
rules.then: Use vstack.

rules.if: Content is inside a navigationstack and represents document-style rows
rules.then: Use list inside navigationstack.

decisionRules.question: How should I group rows inside a list?

rules.if: Rows belong to a named category (Account, Settings, Support)
rules.then: Use section with title inside list.

rules.if: No named grouping is needed
rules.then: Place children directly in list without section.

rules.DO NOT: Use vstack to simulate section groups inside a list.

decisionRules.question: Should I use label or manually compose hstack + icon + text?

rules.if: You need icon + text as a semantic unit (list row, menu item, button content)
rules.then: Always use label.

rules.if: The icon and text need independent styles or unrelated positions
rules.then: Use hstack with icon and text children.

rules.if: Screen uses navigationstack
rules.then: Place toolbaritem as a direct child of navigationstack. Put the button inside toolbaritem as a child.

rules.if: toolbaritem is placed outside navigationstack
rules.then: Its children render flat — no toolbar behavior.

decisionRules.question: Should I use group or section?

rules.if: Grouping rows inside a list
rules.then: Use section.

rules.if: Grouping standalone content outside a list, with optional card/inset style or collapsible behavior
rules.then: Use group.

decisionRules.question: Should I wrap content in scroll?

rules.if: Content may overflow the viewport and is NOT inside a list or tabview
rules.then: Use scroll as the container.

rules.if: Content is a list
rules.then: Do NOT wrap list in scroll — list is already scrollable.

rules.if: Content is inside a tab inside tabview
rules.then: Use scroll inside the tab if the tab content is long.

decisionRules.question: Should I use image or icon?

rules.if: Large standalone image, hero image, or large SF Symbol as a visual element
rules.then: Use image. Set width/height props to constrain the size.

rules.if: Small inline decorative SF Symbol glyph
rules.then: Use icon.

rules.if: Icon + label text together
rules.then: Use label (not icon + text composed manually).

rules.use: vstack for custom vertical layouts that are not row-based lists.
vstack inside a tab's children for custom tab content layout.
vstack for hero/banner sections, form areas, or mixed content without row structure.

rules.DO NOT: Use vstack as a substitute for list, section, group, or navigationstack.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_inside_scroll
antiPatterns.description: Adding a vstack as the only child of a scroll node.
antiPatterns.problem: The renderer already inserts a VStack inside scroll internally. Adding another vstack creates a redundant double-wrap.
antiPatterns.fix: Place content children directly inside scroll without a vstack wrapper.

antiPatterns.name: manual_label_composition
antiPatterns.description: Composing hstack + icon + text instead of using the label node.
antiPatterns.problem: label is a semantic node that correctly expresses icon+text unity and handles platform layout. Manual hstack+icon+text bypasses this.
antiPatterns.fix: Use label with title and icon props whenever icon+text is a single semantic unit.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_as_list
antiPatterns.description: Using vstack to render a list of similar content rows instead of list.
antiPatterns.problem: vstack produces custom layout without list styling. Rows lack separators, inset grouping, and platform-standard list appearance.
antiPatterns.fix: Use list with optional section children for row-based content.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_simulating_sections
antiPatterns.description: Using vstack children inside a list to simulate section groups.
antiPatterns.problem: list's section child provides semantic grouping with a visible header. A vstack inside list renders flat without section behavior.
antiPatterns.fix: Use section with a title prop directly inside list for grouped rows.

antiPatterns.name: toolbaritem_outside_navigationstack
antiPatterns.description: Placing toolbaritem as a child of vstack, hstack, or any container other than navigationstack.
antiPatterns.problem: toolbaritem only gains toolbar behavior when lifted by navigationstack. Outside that context, children render inline.
antiPatterns.fix: toolbaritem must be a direct child of navigationstack.

antiPatterns.name: tab_outside_tabview
antiPatterns.description: Using a tab node outside of a tabview.
antiPatterns.problem: tab renders as a plain VStack with no tabItem modifier when not inside tabview.
antiPatterns.fix: tab nodes must be direct children of tabview.

antiPatterns.name: nested_navigationstack
antiPatterns.description: Placing a navigationstack inside another navigationstack.
antiPatterns.problem: Nested navigation stacks produce conflicting navigation bars and undefined behavior.
antiPatterns.fix: Use a single navigationstack at the appropriate root level.

antiPatterns.name: list_wrapped_in_scroll
antiPatterns.description: Wrapping a list node inside a scroll container.
antiPatterns.problem: list is already a scrollable container. Wrapping it in scroll creates conflicting scroll areas.
antiPatterns.fix: Use list directly. Do not wrap it in scroll.

antiPatterns.name: vstack_root_with_nav_intent
antiPatterns.description: Using vstack as the root when the screen clearly requires a navigation bar, title, or toolbar.
antiPatterns.problem: vstack does not provide navigation chrome. The title and toolbar buttons will be absent.
antiPatterns.fix: Use navigationstack as the root container. Place the vstack content inside navigationstack.

antiPatterns.name: missing_state_declaration
antiPatterns.description: Using a bind prop that references a state key not declared in state.vars.
antiPatterns.problem: The runtime will produce an empty/nil value for the binding. The initial state is undefined.
antiPatterns.fix: Declare every state key referenced in bind props in state.vars with an appropriate initial value.

antiPatterns.name: empty_events_omitted
antiPatterns.description: Omitting the events field when no events are defined.
antiPatterns.problem: events is a required field. Omitting it will cause document validation failure.
antiPatterns.fix: Always include events: {} even when there are no event handlers.

antiPatterns.name: frame_as_group_substitute
antiPatterns.description: Using frame as a general content grouping container instead of group.
antiPatterns.problem: frame is for sizing and alignment constraints. It provides no title, footer, or semantic grouping.
antiPatterns.fix: Use group with the appropriate style prop for semantic content grouping.
